name: Agent depends on live external web data
private_condition: The product cannot complete its core job from internal or static data alone.
observable_proxy: The company launches an agent that researches companies, markets, code, products, or current events from the public web.
rationale: A live-web dependency creates direct need for reliable discovery and extraction.
search_queries: company autonomous agent live web data
company AI research agent launch
company agent public web research product
false_positive_risks: The product may use only internal CRM or licensed database data.
The launch may be a prototype rather than a production dependency.

name: Needs structured page-level extraction
private_condition: Search snippets do not contain the fields downstream agents need.
observable_proxy: Documentation, jobs, or engineering posts mention browser automation, parsing, normalization, or structured extraction.
rationale: Page-level field extraction is a stronger Nimble fit than simple search.
search_queries: company web extraction structured data
browser automation data normalization job
site:company.com careers web data engineer extraction
false_positive_risks: The role may support a one-time migration.
An official API may already satisfy the requirement.

name: Freshness or reliability pain
private_condition: Stale, blocked, or unstable external data harms product quality.
observable_proxy: The company discusses stale retrieval, scraping failures, outdated recommendations, coverage gaps, or provider fragmentation.
rationale: Publicly stated freshness or reliability pain creates stronger urgency than generic web-data usage.
search_queries: company stale data retrieval issue
company scraping reliability challenge
company outdated embeddings current web context
false_positive_risks: The issue may already be resolved.
The complaint may concern an unrelated internal dataset.

name: High-volume or multi-source web workflow
private_condition: The company must collect or refresh information across many pages, domains, or categories.
observable_proxy: The product claims broad coverage, many monitored domains, new market expansion, or high-volume research automation.
rationale: Volume and source diversity increase the value of managed web-intelligence infrastructure.
search_queries: company product monitors thousands of websites
company expands market intelligence coverage
company high volume account research automation
false_positive_risks: Marketing coverage claims may be inflated.
The company may purchase the data from a licensed aggregator.

name: Multiple agents or AI workflows
private_condition: Several agents must coordinate work rather than operate as isolated assistants.
observable_proxy: The company launches several specialized agents or describes multi-agent workflows across departments.
rationale: Multiple agents create ownership, handoff, and shared-context needs.
search_queries: company multi agent workflow launch
company internal AI agents departments
company specialized agents sales support research
false_positive_risks: Several agents may be marketing labels for one underlying chatbot.
The agents may not interact with a human team.

name: Context spread across tools
private_condition: Agents and people repeatedly reconstruct business context across disconnected systems.
observable_proxy: Public architecture or hiring material shows agents working across chat, CRM, email, GitHub, documents, or support systems.
rationale: Cross-system work creates a strong need for a shared organization layer.
search_queries: company agents Slack GitHub Notion CRM
company repeated prompting context AI tools
company AI workflow across CRM email chat
false_positive_risks: Integration breadth does not always imply context fragmentation.
The company may already have a strong internal coordination layer.

name: Human approval or governance requirement
private_condition: Sensitive agent actions cannot execute without review, permissions, and an audit trail.
observable_proxy: Security, product, job, or workflow content mentions human-in-the-loop control, approval, governance, or scoped permissions.
rationale: Approval requirements create a concrete Kylon workflow rather than a generic collaboration pitch.
search_queries: company human in the loop AI agents
company AI agent governance approval
company agent scoped permissions audit trail
false_positive_risks: Approval may apply only to one regulated edge case.
The company may already use an internal approval system.

name: Recent AI organization trigger
private_condition: A launch, hiring push, funding event, or expansion makes the web-data and coordination problem urgent now.
observable_proxy: The company recently launched an agent product, hired AI operations or web-data roles, raised capital, or expanded coverage.
rationale: A recent event establishes timing and prevents static-fit accounts from dominating the pipeline.
search_queries: company AI agent product launch recent
company hiring AI operations web data
company funding multi agent platform
false_positive_risks: A funding or launch event does not prove active purchase evaluation.
The trigger may increase awareness without creating budget.
